title: TC-DFU-LEGEND50-001: Verify Charge Case firmware update completes after language change triggers DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on the paired smartphone.
Navigate to Device screen and tap on 'Poly Voyager Legend 50'.
Go to Settings → Language and change the headset language (e.g., from English to French).
Observe if the app triggers a Device Firmware Update (DFU) prompt for the headset.
If prompted, accept/confirm the firmware update and let it complete.
After the headset DFU completes, navigate to Device → About → Firmware Version.
Note the headset firmware version.
Navigate to Device → Charge Case → Firmware Version.
Note the charge case firmware version.
Compare both firmware versions against the latest available versions on Poly support.
expected: The headset firmware is updated to the latest version after the language-triggered DFU. The charge case firmware version remains at its previously installed version and is NOT inadvertently downgraded, erased, or left in an inconsistent state. The charge case continues to function normally (charging, LED indicators, pairing behavior).

title: TC-DFU-LEGEND50-002: Verify Charge Case functionality post-language-triggered DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to Settings → Language and change the language (e.g., from English to German).
Confirm and complete any triggered DFU for the headset.
After DFU completes and headset reconnects, place the headset into the charge case.
Verify the charge case LED indicates charging (solid or blinking LED per device behavior).
Remove the headset from the charge case and verify the headset powers on and connects to the phone automatically.
Navigate to Device → Charge Case in the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Verify the charge case battery level is displayed correctly (not showing 0%, error, or 'N/A').
Verify the charge case firmware version is displayed and matches the pre-DFU version (unless a separate charge case update was also applied).
expected: The charge case operates normally after the headset language-triggered DFU: LED indicators work, charging functions correctly, the headset pairs and connects when removed from the case, and the Poly Studio Mobile app correctly reports the charge case battery level and firmware version without errors.

title: TC-DFU-LEGEND50-003: Verify charge case firmware version persistence across headset language-triggered DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to Device → About and record the current headset firmware version.
Navigate to Device → Charge Case and record the current charge case firmware version.
Navigate to Settings → Language and change the headset language (e.g., from English to Spanish).
Complete any triggered DFU for the headset.
After DFU completes and device reconnects, navigate to Device → About and record the new headset firmware version.
Navigate to Device → Charge Case and record the charge case firmware version.
Compare the pre-DFU and post-DFU charge case firmware versions.
Verify the headset firmware version has changed (updated).
expected: The headset firmware version is updated after the language-triggered DFU. The charge case firmware version remains unchanged (identical to the pre-DFU version), confirming that the charge case firmware is not affected by a headset-only DFU triggered by a language change.

title: TC-DFU-LEGEND50-004: Verify charge case pairing is not lost after headset language-triggered DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to Settings → Language and change the headset language (e.g., from English to Italian).
Complete any triggered DFU for the headset.
After DFU completes and the headset reconnects to the phone, place the headset into the charge case.
Close the charge case lid (if applicable) and wait 5 seconds.
Open the charge case lid and remove the headset.
Verify the headset automatically reconnects to the paired smartphone.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to Device → Charge Case.
Verify the charge case is detected and its battery level and firmware version are displayed.
expected: The charge case remains paired and recognized by the headset and the Poly Studio Mobile app after the headset language-triggered DFU. The headset reconnects to the phone automatically when removed from the case, and the app correctly displays charge case information without requiring re-pairing.

title: TC-DFU-LEGEND50-005: Verify charge case charging behavior during and after headset language-triggered DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Ensure the Poly Voyager Legend 50 headset and charge case both have >50% battery.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the headset.
Navigate to Settings → Language and change the headset language (e.g., from English to Portuguese).
When the DFU prompt appears, place the headset into the charge case.
Observe the charge case LED behavior while the headset is in the case during DFU.
Complete the DFU process with the headset in the charge case.
After DFU completes, remove the headset from the charge case.
Verify the headset powers on and connects to the phone.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to Device → Charge Case.
Verify the charge case battery level is displayed and has not dropped abnormally (e.g., not showing a sudden drop from 50% to 5%).
expected: The charge case continues to charge the headset normally during and after the language-triggered DFU. The charge case LED indicates correct charging status. The charge case battery level in the app remains consistent and does not show abnormal drain or errors.
